title: The Best Italian Food Box: Pasta, Truffle, Wine, Oil - Authentic Gifts From Italy on Subscription
description: The Best Italian Food Box: Pasta, Truffle, Wine, Oil - Authentic Gifts From Italy on Subscription NOT JUST ANOTHER FOOD BOX We have carefully sourced Enogastronomic products that tell the story of the producers, where they come from and the generations of tradition & knowledge used to produce them here in Abruzzo. In addition to each Tasting Experience Box you will also receive: 1. A leap into the life & culture of our handpicked passionate producers. 2. Stories of historic businesses, as well as young entrepreneurs who had to relocate or restart everything after the 3 earthquakes we had in 2009 (L’Aquila), 2016 (Amatrice) and 2017 (Rigopiano). 3.An explanation of the authentic characteristics behind every hand-made product. Exclusive video & printed recipes to make it simple & fun to enjoy these easy recipes with your family and friends. WHO IS THIS FOR? Our Tasting Experience Boxes are thoughtful gifts that can bring seasonal, locally produced products from the heart of Abruzzo to your family and friends around the world. Deliver a single experience or a full year of the best products we can source for: 1. Indulging yourself with delicacies from the heart of Italy; 2. A special gift for someone important to you who loves authentic Italian food 3. Corporate gifts, which you can personalize with your own branding for your employees, suppliers and clients. Get in contact with us to discuss the details. HELP THE SMALL ARTISANS FROM ITALY Remember that as well as making a unique gift from Italy, you will also be supporting your favorite Italian friends and producers. A REVOLUTIONARY PACKAGING For the packaging, we spent considerable time to source a 100% sustainable, recyclable, secure & beautifully designed cardboard case. This was an unanticipated challenge, but we felt it was very important to deliver our food experience in a sturdy box that could fit different products. In the end, we patented a revolutionary packaging that we will reveal in the next few days. Here is a sneak peek just for you. You will receive our box that encloses 3 separate cases containing the 5 products. Each case will be sanitized and wax sealed, a small detail to ensure the quality of our products and your experience. This also offers you the flexibility to use one case as a gift for someone and keep two cases for yourself. FOOD BOX SUBSCRIPTIONS How does the subscription program work? Once you sign up, you will receive a selection of 4/5 products each 2, 3, or 4 months depending on which subscription option you select (new products every time). The subscription lasts 1 year and you will be billed only every 2,3 or 4 months based on your subscription. ITALIAN FOOD GIFT Can I give a subscription as a gift to someone? Certainly! You can gift a subscription to anyone, for example, your parents, grandparents, or friends. You won’t have to do anything, we will ship to them directly. Check out our gift subscription options and remember to include a gift message on the current form(Only 2m to complete) https://forms.gle/PEmrp4MHN2YFjnTf8 SHIPPING DIRECTLY FROM ITALY What countries do you ship to? We are currently shipping our Food Boxes to: Italy (1-2 days) Europe ** (UK included) (2 days) Canada * (5-7 days) United States* (5-7 days) **Europe (UPS): Austria, Belgium, Croatia, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Ireland, Luxemburg, Netherlands, Portugal, United Kingdom, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden. *We are only shipping the ‘Bella’ Food Box to Canada and US. italian food box italian food subscription box italian food gift boxes Our Food Box Experiences, available at 👇👇👇 https://www.experiencebellavita.com/product-category/food-experience-boxes/

title: How to Make My Papá’s AMAZING Claypot Lamb | Best Italian Meat Recipes
description: In this special ‘Italian Food Traditions’ episode, I’m at my family ‘agriturismo’ (Agriturismo L’assiolo), just outside of Matera, to bring you a video in which my Dad makes a traditional recipe that we call ‘La Pignata’. It’s a very old recipe from this part of Italy that is made with mutton, vegetables and many other delicious ingredients. The most interesting part of it though is the way we slow cook it in a beautiful terracotta pot, which is similar to an amphora, for many hours in a wood fire oven. This is such a special part of our culinary culture and tradition here in Basilicata and I wanted to share it with you here on my channel. Who knows, maybe one day you might make the trip to Matera and try it for yourself! Enjoy! Music (in order) Wholesome by Kevin MacLeod Link: https://incompetech.filmmusic.io/song/5050-wholesome License: http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ Iatta Nera by Ragnatela Folk Band Link: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OoTpbyRI2h4 ⇨ For more recipes every week, don't forget to subscribe: https://cutt.ly/vtLKTTR ⇨ Follow me on Facebook here: https://cutt.ly/byts7Pv #Italianfood #italiantraditions #italianculture #recipes #foodie #letseat #cooking #cookthis #food #hungry #dinner #delicious #mutton #lamb

title: Mysore Pak Recipe
description: Short video on how to make Mysore Pak. :) Ingredients: Gram Flour (Besan) - 1 Cup Sugar - 1 Cup Ghee - 1.5 Cups (Melted) Water - 1/2 Cup Recipe: 1. Grease a tray with Ghee and keep aside. 2. Dry roast 1 cup of Gram Flour (Besan) in a pan till it turns golden in colour and aromatic and take it out on a bowl. 3. To that add 1/2 cup of Melted Ghee and mix well without lumps using a whisk. 4. Place a pan on flame and add 1 cup of sugar and 1/2 cup of water. 5. Keep stirring on low flame till you get one string consistency sugar syrup. 6. Then add the Besan-Ghee mixture and keep stirring on low flame. 7. Keep adding the remaining ghee for every few seconds once it starts absorbing. Do not add whole ghee at a time. Add little by little till it absorbs. 8. Keep stirring continuously till it starts seperating the pan and forms a thick halwa like mixture with bubbly texture on top. 9. Turn off the flame and add this mixture on top of the grease tray and spread and pat the tray. 10. Allow it to cool for about 30 minutes and then cut into pieces and enjoy!:) #shortcookigvideo #shorts #mysorepak Music: Funny day Musician: Ilya Truhanov URL: https://icons8.com/music/

Compostable bags for food waste recycling in Italy
description: About one third of the worlds food is thrown out! Food waste is a massive environmental and economic challenge. This channel is dedicated to sharing practical examples of how cities are reducing and recycling food waste. This video is about how Italy supplies compostable bags for food waste recycling via supermarkets (and this is mandated by the government). This distribution model has a number of advantages, which I explain here. After making this video I found out an extra piece of information that there was kick-back with the introduction of the legislation due to the requirement that customers cannot bring their own reusable bags when shopping for fruit and veg. A pitfall to avoid! Detailed case studies and expert interviews on best practice food waste management at: www.beyondfoodwaste.com To sign up and receive food waste stories and interviews, join the mailing list here: https://mailchi.mp/8613565c9c3f/beyond-food-waste #BeyondFoodWaste #foodwaste

title: How to make tiramisu | Italian vegan recipe
description: You can find this vegan tiramisu recipe below. You can find the version without music here: https://youtu.be/2B1adGSRwi0 INGREDIENTS FOR 6 PEOPLE For the vegan mascarpone cream 400 g unsweetened soy yogurt 200 g silken tofu 100 g vegan butter 100 g sugar 50 g soy milk For the vegan sponge cake 200 g all purpose flour 200 g water 180 g sugar 100 g corn starch 100 g refined coconut oil or vegan butter 16 g baking soda 1 vanilla bean 250 ml coffee 1 tbsp sugar 30 g cacao powder INSTRUCTIONS Prepare the mascarpone cream 1. put the yoghurt to drain for at least 8 hours in a sieve wrapped in a cheese cloth with weights on top (better if the night before) 2. with an immersion blender, emulsify the soy milk with the melted vegetable butter 3. blend together all the ingredients for the mascarpone cream (dried yoghurt, tofu, butter and milk emulsion, and sugar). If you use whole-wheat sugar, blend it first separately) Prepare the vegan sponge cake 1. in a bowl, mix water, refined coconut oil, vanilla and sugar 2. pass the all-purpose flour, cornstarch and baking powder through a sieve and add them to the liquid mixture, stirring with a spatula until the mixture is smooth 3. bake in the oven for about 30 / 40 minutes at 180˚C Assemble the tiramisu in layers as shown in the video or as you like. Website: www.theplantbasedschool.com Pinterest: @italiaplantbased Instagram: @italiaplantbased Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/italiaplantbased

title: VEGAN IN VENICE, ITALY // WATCH ME #5
description: Hello beautiful people :) Yes, I know this took forever to finish, but I currently don't even have any editing software at home, soooo...things are tough! However!!! I went to Italy and it was beautiful! Now, Venice is a gorgeous city! But I gotta tell ya: Not the easiest place to find vegan food. If you are looking for good vegan Italien food, I would recommend some of the bigger cities more down south (the more south, the less eggs). The food we did have, was pretty damn nice tough :) When going to Venice (or any city, really) I suggest downloading the Happy Cow app. It's usually spot on. Also look for smaller shops like the one where I got that vegetable dish. They will suddenly appear in front of you and feature some genuine italian cooking. If you are travelling with non-vegans (or you don't like to spend a lot of time researching): go for bruscetta, grilled veggies and pizza marinara. You will get that pretty much anywhere. Stay away from pasta dishes (eggs in the north)! Also, if you do your research on Venice...Every site will recommend "La Zucca" for vegan food. Don't. Skip it. So. Not. Worth. It. I spent 20 € on the same plate of food I had the day before for 4 €!! Enjoy espresso, but always get it at the bar (1€) and never sit down to drink it. Take the city's bus boats instead of a gondula ride if you want to save some cash. Look for "free" walking tours through Venice (just google it, something will come up) for a free tour of Venice. Ours was really great and technically it's free but naturally you donate to your guide however much you can afford. Still, you will enjoy a lovely and personal tour :) Enjoy Venice, it is absolutely BEAUTIFUL. Please take about 10000000000 pictures :)

Pasta, Pane, Vino by Matt Goulding | On Sale June 12
description: “Italy is a beautiful but complicated place, not so much a country as a collection of cultures and cuisines. Matt Goulding expertly navigates it’s wonders and eccentricities with wisdom and great passion.” -Anthony Bourdain "Goulding is pioneering a new type of writing about food." -Financial Times This is not a cookbook. This is something more: a travelogue, a patient investigation of Italy’s cuisine, a loving profile of the everyday heroes who bring Italy to the table. Pasta, Pane, Vino is the latest edition of the genre-bending Roads & Kingdoms style pioneered under Anthony Bourdain’s imprint in Rice, Noodle, Fish ( 2016 Travel Book of the Year, Society of American Travel Writers ) and Grape, Olive, Pig ( 2017 IACP Award, Literary Food Writing). Order your copy of Pasta, Pane, Vino online here: https://goo.gl/Txteyv Town by town, bite by bite, author Matt Goulding brings Italy to life through intimate portraits of its food culture and the people pushing it in new directions: Three globe-trotting brothers who became the mozzarella kings of Puglia; the pizza police of Naples and the innovative pies that stay one step ahead of the rules; the Barolo Boys who turned the hilly Piedmont into one of the world’s great wine regions. Goulding’s writing has never been better, in complete harmony with the book's innovative design and the more than 200 lush color photographs that introduce the chefs, shepherds, fisherman, farmers, grandmas, and guardians who power this country’s extraordinary culinary traditions. From the pasta temples of Rome to the multicultural markets of Sicily to the family-run, fish-driven trattorias of Lake Como, Pasta, Pane, Vino captures the breathtaking diversity of Italian regional food culture. title: Vegan Fettuccine Alfredo Recipe
description: Hello YouTube! Today the #EatingPassport stamps us in Italy! Here I have a Vegan Fettuccine Alfredo!⁣ ⁣⁣ This recipe is for 5 Servings of 620 kCal each.⁣ ⁣ For the Pasta:⁣ 2 Tbsp Olive Oil⁣ 16 Oz Fettuccine Pasta⁣ For the Cashew Sauce:⁣ 2 Garlic Bulbs2 Tbsp Olive Oil1 Cup Raw Cashews1 Cup Coconut milk2 Tbsp Lime Juice1 Tbsp Salt⁣ For the Mushrooms:2 Tbsp Olive Oil12 Oz Baby Portobello Mushrooms⁣ 1 Large Sweet Onion1 Garlic Clove1/4 Cup Cooking WineSalt and Pepper to taste ⁣ ⁣⁣ 1. Pre-heat the oven to 400*F. Cut the tops of the garlic bulbs off, place olive oil in each, and wrap them in tin foil. Place in oven for 40 minutes.⁣ 2. Place a cup of cashews in a pot with water and boil for 15-20 minutes to soften them, or let them soak for 4-8 hours before.⁣ 3. Fill a pot with water and bring to a boil. Add the pasta and salt, cook to instructions, and drain.⁣ 4. In a pan with medium high heat, add the olive oil, the portobello mushrooms, diced onion, and minced garlic clove. Reduce the water content, add the cooking wine and reduce.⁣ 5. In a processor add the oven roasted garlic by squeezing them out after cooling, the softened cashews, the coconut milk, lime juice, and salt. Blend until sauce is completely smooth.⁣ 6. Add the pasta back to the pot, olive oil and mix. Then add in the cashew sauce, the sautéed portobello mushrooms, and mix.⁣ 7. Let heat evenly. Serve and enjoy!⁣ ⁣⁣ Note: Make sure to leave lid of the pot of boiling pasta open to breath sufficiently and prevent overflow spilling of the bubbles.⁣ ⁣⁣ #mealprep #mealplan #mealplanning #vegan #vegetarian #vegetables #fruits #wholefoods #plantbased #recipe #healthyrecipes #freshfood #breakfast #lunch #dinner #global #cuisine #northamerica #centralamerica #southamerica #caribbean #europe #middleeast #africa #asia #australia #veganpasta #veganfettuccine #veganalfredo

title: Italian inspired VEGAN comfort food | RECIPE
description: ROASTED BUTTERNUT SQUASH SAUCE INGREDIENTS 1 tablespoon grape seed oil 1/2 yellow onion, chopped 1 clove garlic, minced 1 cup organic sweet plum tomatoes in jar or canned. (can sub whole roma tomatoes canned.) 1 cup roasted butternut squash 1 medium carrot 2 tablespoons fresh Italian herbs - equal parts (basil, oregano, rosemary, thyme, savory). May use dried herbs 1/4 cup water (can sub pasta water) 1 teaspoon salt 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon 4 teaspoons pure maple syrup 1/2 cup organic plain unsweetened coconut creamer 1/2 cup vegan grated Parmesan (I’d recommend Go Veggie brand or Violife foods.) 1 teaspoon olive oil and some fresh herbs for garnish METHOD Pour the grape seed oil into a large pot over medium heat, and sauté the onion until tender, about 10 minutes. Add in the garlic and fresh herbs. Sauté until fragrant, about 1 more minute. Add in the tomato, pumpkin, vegan parmesan, water, salt, cinnamon, and maple syrup. Bring the sauce to a boil. Once boiling, lower the heat and stir in the coconut milk and minced sage. At this point, let cool and use a blender or food processor to blend to desired texture. Adjust any seasoning to taste and serve warm over your favorite pasta. (I’d recommend preparing a pound of pasta for this amount of sauce.) Add fresh basil and extra virgin olive oil to plate, if so desired. _ Butternut squash is high in vitamin A (beta carotene), potassium, magnesium, and fiber💚 _ HOW TO ROAST BUTTERNUT SQUASH (HALVED) Ingredients: 1. Butternut squash 2. Olive oil or vegan Butter 3. Salt and pepper 4. Fresh herbs (optional) 5. Brown sugar (optional) (Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.) 1. Using a large, sturdy knife, cut your butternut squash in half lengthwise. (Be careful) 2. Scoop out the seeds and stringy bits from each half and discard (or save for roasting). 3. Transfer the butternut squash, cut-side up into a baking dish and brush with olive oil or melted vegan butter, coating the entire surface of the squash. 4. Season with salt and pepper and brown sugar (if desired). 5. Roast in the oven for approximately 45 minutes, or until soft, and fork-tender. 6. Remove from oven and allow to cool. Scoop flesh from the skin and enjoy plain or in your favorite recipes. _ GOURMET PINE NUT CASHEW SPREAD WITH FRESH HERBS Ingredients: 1 package of mozzarella or chive Miyoko's 1/2 cup toasted pine nuts 1 tbsp Earth Balance Organic Whipped Spread (can sub grape seed oil.) 1 heaping tbsp of finely chopped fresh herbs - (Rosemary, oregano, basil) 1/2 tbsp dried savory herb 1 tsp pink salt 1 tsp nutritional yeast 2 tbsp raw agave Method: Open package of cheeze, and set aside inside a medium sized bowl. Use a small to medium skillet and place on stove. Apply low heat. Add vegan butter or grape seed oil and pine nuts to warm skillet. Allow pine-nuts to achieve a golden brown color . Cooking time between 1 - 2 minutes to achieve this. Place skillet contents over the cheeze in bowl. Add the agave, herbs, salt, and nutritional yeast. Mix ingredients until everything is combined evenly throughout the cheeze spread. Serve immediately. Makes 4-8 servings. Stays fresh in airtight containers refrigerated for 2 weeks. Excellent to add a gourmet touch to your favorite pastas and homemade pizzas. Serves as a luxurious antipasto (appetizer) to surprise your friends and family, they’ll never be able to tell it’s #vegan! _ This event was hosted by our good friend, founder of Adventures of Will, retreat host, vlogger, and real estate powerhouse, Will Edmond. == Follow Will Edmond == ★ Facebook - http://on.fb.me/1ICqNfm ★ Instagram - http://bit.ly/WillEdmondInstagram ★ Travel Site - http://www.willedmond.com _ This video was shot by Will Edmond, also a camera wiz, and Keyra Correa, aka humble vegan warrior. Edited by Keyra Correa. title: Nutri-Score helps consumers to make the healthiest choice!
description: Wondering what our MEPs are doing in a supermarket? title: New poll proves being vegan isn't as expensive as you think!
description: New poll proves being vegan isn't as expensive as you think! https://youtu.be/WuIJKfB3W5w According to Forbes, a recent Gallup poll found that the majority of vegetarians and vegans in the United States made less than $30,000 per year, classifying them as low income by federal standards. What’s more, the survey found that meat consumption was greater among higher-income people. Those earning more than $70,000 annually were least likely to have veg diets, with 5 percent being vegetarian and only 2 percent being vegan. This flies in the face of the belief that a plant-based diet is expensive and only achievable for the affluent. Read the blog post here: https://mercyforanimals.org/gallup-poll-americans-making-less-than-30k Make sure not to miss our future videos! title: 1700 Vegan Recipes. The very best vegan cookbook in the whole world is "Det veganska köket"
description: Pelle Agorelius recieving the certificate for the best vegan cookbook in the world on behalf of the author and publisher Mattias Kristiansson, on stage at the Gourmand Awards in Macao, july 4th with Gourmand Awards founder and president Edouard Cointreau. Mattias Kristiansson says he wrote the vegan food bible “Det veganska köket” to really show the width of the vegan cuisine. It's the only basic cookbook you need if you are interested in vegan cooking, with accurate recipes, everyday food, baking, nutritions facts, desserts and more. The book is divided in chapters like beans, lentils, grains, vegetables, fruits, raw food, spices and so on. A simple design with beautiful photos to show that vegan food os much more than a salad and hummus. This vegan food bible the Gourmand Awards winning (1st prize, category Vegan), full title in swedish is "Det veganska köket" and is published by Vegoriket AB. Author Mattias Kristiansson is one of the biggest names in Sweden when it comes to vegan food. He founded Swedens first all vegetarian and vegan food magazine in 2014 and have published 10 cookbooks. He has been cooking food on both tv and radio, always with a big smile and a dose of humor. His goal is to inspire as many people as possible to eat more vegan and he does so with simple and delicious recipes that everyone can cook from. He want vegan food to be fun and something that everyone feels that they can handle and want to try. Mattias is continuously finding new ways to reach out to people, whether it's with his on podcast, youtube-videos, instagram, facebook and lectures. A star is born.

title: Fusillo Italian Restaurant review, Vegetarian and Vegan Food in Pattaya, Reviewed by Kelly Veg
description: Fusillo Italian Restaurant review in Pattaya (Italian) Happily recommended but best you try for yourself. (Vegetarian and Vegan Friendly Restaurant that services omnivores also) Read on for the informative expanded written review. 157/62-63 Pattaya Naklua Road, Chonburi 20150 On the west side of Naklua Road a bit South of Soi 18 091-768-7766 Very friendly and informative Husband & Wife - Manager & Owner = Francesco Pagano & Nathanon Poyantuk Facebook https://www.facebook.com/Fusillo-Italian-restaurant-and-pizza-482445645471219/ Listed in Facebook group: Vegetarian or Vegan Friendly Restaurants in Pattaya in Thailand & Beyond https://web.facebook.com/groups/830057863791637/ (Your joining is appreciated.) Listed at Youtube Channel Kelly Veg https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCWXzZFoZ3wVEJNsZYvfwssQ (Your subscribing is appreciated.) They import many ingredients from Italy. Their yogurt is organic made in house. Their Italian Style Ice Cream is made locally to their specifications. For full flavor they cut the vegetables on the spot when making the order. No pre-cutting and pre-preparing. 28 padded chairs inside / 28 on patio A/C Nice décor Minestrone Soup 220b = Great flavor - Parmesan Cheese on the side - huge bowl loaded with fresh cut veggies, cauliflower, broccoli, carrot, peas, green beans, tomato, potato, onion, red pepper – they do not use macaroni as a cheap filler like many restaurants do, instead it is jam packed with veggies – it could be a meal in itself! Gnocchi alla Sorrentina 250b = cheese, tomato sauce, basil – gnocchi were cooked to perfection – the tomato sauce tasted delicious and was plentiful – did not skimp on the cheese plus parmesan cheese on the side – good sized portion Lemon Ice Cream (1 scoop 50b) = fantastic flavor, creamy, smooth Healthy Breakfast 250b = organic yogurt, muesli, fresh fruit, milk, orange juice Frittata Omelet 170b Uova Monzerella e Pomodori 180b = eggs, cheese, tomato sauce Verdure Fritte in Pastella 150b = breaded fried vegetables Risotto with Mushrooms 320b Gnocchi al Gorgonzola 250b Spinach Ravioli with Basil & Tomato Sauce 260b Spinach Ravioli with Gorgonzola Cheese 260b Ravioli Mushroom & Cream 280b Cannelloni Ricotta 250b Penne all’ Arrabbiata 200b Fettuccini Pesto 250b Spaghetti Tomato & Basil 200b Lentil and Potato Soup 250b = (vegetarians ask for no bacon in it) Vegetarian Pizza 270b Margherita Pizza 220b Mushroom Pizza 240b 4 Cheese Pizza 280b Cheese Plate 400b Italian Ice Cream – multiple flavors – 1 scoop 50b / 2 scoops 90b / 3 scoops 120b Desserts 90b to 200b Apple Cake 90b Fruit Shakes 80b Juices 50b to 70b 9:30am – kitchen closes at 10pm but if customers still arriving they sometimes stay open longer. (They are closed for the month of April each year.) NOTE: My reviews are honest and not coerced by the restaurants. This review is subjective based on my experience with only a few menu items. Your experience may be different than mine. You can support me in creating more reviews through your likes, shares, subscriptions and comments. They are welcomed and appreciated. title: Vegan Mushroom Polenta Recipe
description: Total Time: 30 minutes Ingredients 1 cup instant polenta 400 grams Brussels sprouts, trimmed and quartered 100 grams assorted wild mushrooms (fresh, not dried) 2 tablespoons olive oil 4 cups water 3/4 cup shredded Parmesan cheese, divided 1/4 cup chopped fresh sage Salt and pepper, to taste Method Preheat oven to 200ºC. Toss the Brussels sprouts and mushrooms in a bowl with the olive oil, salt, and pepper. Transfer to a baking sheet lined oven tray and bake for about 20 minutes, or until nicely browned. While the veggies are roasting, boil water in a large saucepan. Reduce the heat to low and slowly whisk in the polenta and continue to whisk constantly until the polenta is thickened, about 3 minutes. Remove from heat and stir in 1/2 cup of Parmesan, sage, and salt and pepper to taste. Divide the polenta into 3-4 bowls and top each with 1/4 of the roasted veggies and 1 tablespoon of Parmesan cheese. https://www.goagainsttheflow.org.au/vegan-polenta-mushrooms/

title: Bella Italia Restaurant review, Italian Vegetarian and Vegan Food in Pattaya, Reviewed by Kelly Veg
description: Bella Italia Restaurant review in Pattaya (Italian, Thai) Happily recommended food but best to try for yourself. (Vegetarian and vegan friendly restaurant that also services omnivores.) Read on for the informative expanded written review. 78/13 Moo 9, 2nd Road Nongprue Banglamung Chonburi, Pattaya, Thailand 20260 On the east side of 2nd Road across from Soi 6 089-752-2172 list.bellaitaliapattaya@gmail.com Website http://www.bellaitaliapattaya.com/ Facebook https://www.facebook.com/BLIpattaya/ Listed in the Facebook group: Vegetarian or Vegan Friendly Restaurants in Pattaya in Thailand & Beyond https://web.facebook.com/groups/830057863791637/ (Your joining is appreciated.) This restaurant’s video review is listed along with many others at the YouTube Channel named Kelly Veg https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCWXzZFoZ3wVEJNsZYvfwssQ (Your subscribing is appreciated.) The Bella Italia Restaurant video review is posted on YouTube https://youtu.be/u_Q6AFeTgSc (Open in YouTube to see the written text with links to the location / contact information / menu items / and a more extensive written review.)(Likes, comments, shares and subscriptions are welcomed and appreciated.) Nice décor A/C inside – fans on Patio Seating for big groups Excellent service Nice touch – gratuitous watermelon & pineapple given at the end Minestrone Soup 120b = not traditional style but delicious (no tomato, thankfully no white flour macaroni) – loaded with fresh cut vegetables including onions, carrots, peas, leeks, spinach, mushrooms, kidney beans & chick peas – good sized bowl Gnocchi 250b = by requested substitute from menu listing to vegetarian style, tomato sauce & basil – Most pastas are made with white flour which I am not fond of for health reasons so it is nice to have Gnocchi made mostly with potato – cooked to a nice consistency – good sized portion with a generous amount of the great tasting sauce – parmesan cheese on the side Ravioli Spinach 240b = very yummy – bowl of parmesan on the side – good sized portion Spinach Lasagna 285b = very good – lots of spinach & cheese, light on the pasta – filling – no tomato sauce though but they did bring me some by request. Watermelon Shake 90b = fresh very tasty – came with a slice of watermelon – medium sized glass Lemon Ice Cream 50b per scoop = this was the only disappointing item I have had at Bella Italia – it came out looking great, large scoop of ice cream with whipping cream, chocolate sauce drizzled on and topped off with a cherry – but this was no robust well flavored Italian style ice cream – instead it was a low quality ice cream with what tasted like artificial lemon flavor. Vegetarian Salads 120b to 250b Veggie Pizzas 150b to 330b Vegetarian Spaghetti dishes 180b to 240b Penne Arrabbiata 210b Penne Fresco Estate 240b Spinach Ravioli with Cheese Sauce 330b Pan Fried Vegetables 140b Pan Fried Mixed Mushrooms 150b Bruschetta Caprese 140b Bruschetta Spinach & Cheese 120b Caprese 290b Mashed Potato 100b Fries 100b Desserts 70b to 150b Ice Cream 50b per scoop Fruit Juices (canned) 75b Fruit Juices (fresh) 110b Shakes 90b 3:30pm to 12am (last order at 11:30pm) NOTE: My reviews are honest and not coerced by the restaurants. This review is subjective based on my experience with only a few menu items. Your experience may be different than mine. title: How to make VEGAN CUSTARD - The Ultimate Video GUIDE!
description: Ingredients for all our custard recipes below. Captions in English and Italian :-) 0:00 Low fat vegan custard 2:27 Full-fat vegan custard 3:20 Coffee flavoured custard 3:53 Pistachio Custard 4:53 Chocolate Custard PRINT RECIPES → How to make custard guide: https://theplantbasedschool.com/vegan-custard/ → Coffee custard: https://theplantbasedschool.com/coffee-custard/ → Pistachio custard: https://theplantbasedschool.com/pistachio-custard/ → Chocolate custard: https://theplantbasedschool.com/chocolate-custard/ JOIN THE COMMUNITY ■ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/italiaplantbased/ ■ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/italiaplantbased ■ Pinterest: https://www.pinterest.com/italiaplantbased INGREDIENTS LIST for 6 Servings 🌱 Low-Fat Custard 500 g (2.1 cups) plant-milk soy, almon, oats, rice 50 g (6.7 tbsp) cornstarch 65 g (5.2 tbsp) sugar ⅛ tsp turmeric 1 tsp vanilla extract ¼ lemon peel 🌱 Full Fat Custard 350 g (1.5 cups) plant-milk soy, almond, oats, rice 150 g (0.6 cups) plant-cream soy cream, rice cream, coconut cream or coconut milk 50 g (6.7 tbsp) cornstarch 65 g (5.2 ) sugar ⅛ tsp turmeric 1 tsp vanilla extract 🌱 Coffee Custard 500 g (2.1 cups) plant milk soy, almond, rice, oat 80 g (6.5 tbsp) sugar 50 g (6.7 tbsp) cornstarch 2 tbsp instant coffee powder or ½ cup of espresso coffee 1 tsp vanilla extract 🌱 Pistachio Custard 500 g (2.1 cups) plant milk soy, almond, rice, oat 55 g (1.9 oz) pistachios unsalted 65 g (5 tbsp) sugar 30 g (4 tbsp) cornstarch 🌱 Chocolate Custard 500 g (2.1 cups) plant milk soy, almond, rice, oat 75 g (6 tbsp) sugar 40-50 g (5.5 tbsp) cornstarch 60 g (2.1 oz) dark chocolate 1 tsp vanilla extract These recipes are 100% plant-based (vegan), and so without milk, without butter, without cheese, without eggs, and without any animal-based products. Contacts ✉️ Nico: nico@theplantbasedschool.com ✉️ Louise: louise@theplantbasedschool.com Italia Plant Based Filmed and edited by Louise. Made with ❤ in our little Italian kitchen. Nico :-)

title: VEGAN STUFFED PEPPERS WITH RICE
description: Ingredients for Vegan Stuffed Peppers with Rice below. These stuffed Bell Peppers are 100% plant-based (vegan), and so free of milk, free of butter, free of cheese, and free from any animal based products. PRINTABLE RECIPE https://theplantbasedschool.com/vegan-stuffed-bell-peppers-with-rice/ FOLLOW US ■ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/italiaplantbased/ ■ Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/italiaplantbased ■ Pinterest: https://www.pinterest.com/italiaplantbased INGREDIENTS 🌱 Vegan Stuffed Bell Peppers 1.5 cups rice (280 grams) 5 whole red bell peppers 4 whole tomatoes 1 whole onion 2 cloves garlic 2 tbsp tomato paste 3 tbsp extra virgin olive oil 2 tsp sea salt 1 tsp paprika 1 tsp pepper 1 handful flat-leaf parsley (40 grams) 1 handful cilantro (40 grams) 0.5 handful dill (20 grams) 2 tbsp vegetable bouillon powder 1 litre water for the vegetable broth Contacts ✉️ Nico: nico@theplantbasedschool.com ✉️ Louise: louise@theplantbasedschool.com Italia Plant Based Made with ❤ in our little Italian kitchen. Nico :-)

title: Bella Italia - "Plant Based Carpaccio" with Healthy Brown Rice Risotto and "Dairy Free Panna Cotta"
description: Welcome Back to YouRoots! We are excited that you’ve decided to tune in and hear what we have to stay. We cook, wholesome, nutritious and delicious plant based foods. Our goal is to help you incorporate more plants into your daily life. We encourage you to eat as much wholesome wholefoods as you can but realize this might not be realistic for everyone. We do not want you to have an “all or nothing” attitude, even if you have to make small changes to start, it will be a change in the right direction! For most people an 80/20 is the most realistic to follow and we definitely encourage that, eating 80% wholesome wholefoods and getting a little “crazy” 20% of the time. Of course 100% is most optimal. In today’s episode we are going to Italy - Bella Italia! We are doing a plant based take on some traditional Italian dishes, the final product turned out amazingly and are packed full of nutritious, delicious wholefoods! Just the way we like it at the Back To YouRoots kitchen! The menu for today involves three dishes - Tomato Carpaccio with Healthy Brown Rice Risotto and Dairy Free Panna Cotta! The dessert was definitely the star of the show considering how simplistic it was to make, but isn't dessert always the best part, especially when it's made with minimal ingredients? As a friendly reminder, no one was hurt in the making of this episode, aside from the veggies, they were murdered. For the list of wholesome ingredients, recipe and detailed instruction on how to cook everything, please visit our blog at www.backtoyouroots.com. Be sure to give this video a thumbs up if you enjoyed it! If you have any feedback or comments, good or bad, please let us know below, we welcome all feedback as we are always looking to improve. From the both of us remember to be positive, live with purpose and eat nutritious and delicious wholesome plant based foods! Remember to subscribe for more content - have a legendary day! Feel free to share this video by using the following link: https://youtu.be/kKStDYznvq0 Conrad & Chef Bernie

title: Recipe: Brian’s “Mom-Inspired” Italian Stuffed Mushrooms (Plant-Based, Vegan)
description: Find a printable version of the recipe & more info here: https://bit.ly/KrockMushrooms Apparently it’s Mushroom Week over here in our kitchen and we’ve got another mushroom video for you! We’ve been working on perfecting this recipe for a while after being inspired by a new recipe we tried out recently and an old favorite from Brian’s childhood. The result was a tasty recipe that even this former mushroom-hater enjoys! The idea for this recipe started when I decided to make the Sriracha Stuffed Mushrooms from our favorite book, The Healthspan Solution. Side Note: They were extremely delicious and I highly recommend checking out both the recipe and the book! For Brian, they brought back memories of another recipe his mom used to make. Of course he immediately started thinking of how he could use a similar method to recreate his old favorite… and thus Brian’s “Mom-Inspired” Italian Stuffed Mushrooms were born! ---- Visit our Amazon page for all of our favorite products: https://www.amazon.com/shop/krocksinthekitchen PLEASE NOTE: If you choose to purchase anything through this link, we receive a small commission at NO extra cost to you. title: Food for Thoughts | Carne Plant Based, Vivi intervista Alberto Musacchio
description: La deliziosa carne plant based, a base vegetale, è da poco sbarcata nei menù Vivi. Cristina Cattaneo, co-fondatrice del marchio Vivi, ne parla con Alberto Musacchio, uno dei geniali produttori italiani che fornisce la carne a base vegetale ai nostri Bistrot. Perché la carne plant based è amica dell'ambiente, come fanno delle fibre vegetali ad avere un delizioso sapore di carne e molto altro sul mondo del gusto e del basso impatto ambientale. Se vuoi scoprire di più sulla carne plant based, visita il nostro blog: https://www.vivibistrot.com/blog/

title: Plant Based Meals - Pizza ! Homemade (Vegan)
description: Learn how to make a Plant Based Pizza right at Home! In this Episode of Plant Based NM , we get a hand from one of our subscribers The Cooking Gal 23263, and we are going to give the ”Home Made Pizza” thing a shot this weekend ! Some great PlantBased toppings we like RedOnion Mushroom Artichoce Hearts BlackOlives GreenChile Daiya on top of some pizza sauce , seasoned with garlic and oregano..In the oven for 10-15 minutes. If you’re having trouble achieving a nice crispy crust, or maybe your pizza is a bit soggy In the middle , try putting a little less sauce or baking a little longer than suggested. A little drizzle of raw olive oil once out of the oven makes for a nice touch of Italian flavor to your pizza/crust . The crust is really what makes the pizza :) Ingredients: Trader Joe's Premade Pizza Crust Maranara Sauce or Pizza Sauce Non Dairy Mozzarella "Cheese" Chopped Mushroom Chopped Red Onion Spinach Fresh Green Chile Season with Garlic and Oregano !PLANT BASED PARMESAN CHEESE RECIPE ! https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hat8TDb0TrQ

title: VERY BEST LENTIL SOUP | vegetarian one-pot lentil soup recipe
description: Lentil soup is a classic vegetarian soup recipe. It's hearty, healthy, delightfully filling (with plant-based protein), and warming on those cold, wintery days. While there are many variations of lentil soup, you can't go wrong with a simple, Mediterranean version. I always love an easy, one-pot soup recipe like this as it's easy to meal prep and reheat. It's perfect for lunch or dinner and it's naturally gluten-free, vegan, and dairy-free, making it perfect for almost all your guests! 🖨 Printable Lentil Soup recipe: https://downshiftology.com/recipes/very-best-lentil-soup/ Sign up for weekly emails (never miss a recipe!): https://mailchi.mp/downshiftology/youtube/ Join the private Facebook group of fellow Downshifters around the world: https://www.facebook.com/groups/downshiftologycommunity/ ► PRODUCTS MENTIONED: Vitamix Immersion Blender: https://amzn.to/36Xoenc Round Dutch Oven: https://amzn.to/2IPzA4F Silicone Utensils: https://bit.ly/37z5auN Green Lentils: https://amzn.to/39dpZPA Soup Bowls (similar): https://bit.ly/3pQTOLL Lemon Juicer: https://amzn.to/3pZkf2f ► ALWAYS IN MY KITCHEN: Counter Stools: http://bit.ly/2tUNvZG Vitamix Blender: https://amzn.to/2NjFp75 Spiralizer: https://amzn.to/2N37WRy Tea Kettle: http://amzn.to/2uuNSMp Utensil Holder: http://bit.ly/2tVfGaH Flour Jars: https://amzn.to/30UZESn Salt Cellar: https://amzn.to/2UUyK9r Knife Set: http://bit.ly/2tUGzMa Cutting Board: https://amzn.to/2Vskgv9 Glass Mixing Bowls: https://amzn.to/2L2hx6O Magnetic Measuring Spoons: https://amzn.to/2YIeKYT Magnetic Measuring Cups: https://amzn.to/3hAJu6A For everything else I use in my videos, check out the SHOP page on my website: https://downshiftology.com/shop/ ► TIMESTAMPS: 00:00 Intro 00:47 Slice vegetables for mirepoix 02:38 Add mirepoix vegetables to a large pot with oil and saute 03:02 Add tomato paste, garlic, and spices 03:35 Add diced tomatoes, broth, and lentils, then simmer 04:24 Juice lemon and slice kale leaves 04:56 Overview of the Vitamix Immersion Blender 06:08 Use the immersion blender to create a cremier texture 06:39 Add kale, and lemon juice 06:59 Serve the soup into bowls or meal prep and store 07:50 Taste test ► WHAT I'M WEARING Long Sleeve Tshirt (similar): https://bit.ly/3m0FmyM Jeans: http://bit.ly/32Fq7l5 Linen Apron: http://bit.ly/2twA32E The music I use: https://bit.ly/2Ms5nF2 - Great music for YouTubers! title: Recipe: Italian Vegetable Bean Soup aka “Weird Orange Soup” (Vegan, Oil-Free)
description: Read our blog post for more info, photos & links to products used in this video: http://bit.ly/KrockWOS One of our staple recipes over the past year has been what we lovingly call our “Weird Green Soup.” Since we make it so often, we’ve had a chance to play around with the recipe quite a bit! One of our favorite variations is an Italian inspired version, so we thought we’d share it with you. This one turned out with a mostly orange hue, so we decided to dub it our “Weird Orange Soup.” The original inspiration for this recipe was the "Too-Busy-to-Cook Vegetable Bean Soup" from page 147 of Dr. Fuhrman's Eat To Live Quick & Easy Cookbook. Weird Green Soup Video: https://youtu.be/o62UuFZdP7I For more about our 280+ pound weight loss journey, check out our Weight Loss Playlist: http://bit.ly/KrockWeight Check out our friend Martin The Great Potato Mage on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/MartintheGreatPotatoMage He started his diet with plain potatoes and has switched to a mostly whole food, plant-based diet as well. title: VEGAN LENTIL FLATBREAD » Gluten-Free, Oil Free, Super Simple to Make!
channel: The Whole Food Plant Based Cooking Show
description: Curries and stews just aren't the same without a nice piece of flatbread to dip with. This plant based vegan lentil flatbread recipe couldn't be any simpler. With just two ingredients, these are super quick to whip up for any meal of the day. They work great for sopping up the gravy left in your bowl or as a bread substitute for peanut butter and jelly sandwiches! title: No Oil Whole Food Plant Based Stuffed Bell Peppers
description: In this video I show you how to make no oil whole food plant based bell peppers. Super easy, healthy and delicious! Let get started on these no oil whole food plant based stuffed peppers. Ingredients: 4 to 5 bell peppers seeded and sliced open per your preference 1/2 chopped onions 2 cloves garlic minced 4 mushrooms sliced 1 14.5 oz. can of diced Italian tomatoes 1 cup cooked rice 3/4 cup drained and rinsed garbanzo beans 1 tsp. dry parsley 1 tsp. Italian seasoning 1/2 tsp. onion powder 1/2 to 1 tsp salt pepper to taste panko bread crumbs veggie broth for sauteing veggies or you can use water. plant based cheese sauce (link below) Saute onion in water or veggie broth for a couple of minutes, add garlic and saute for another couple of minutes adding water or broth as needed. Add mushrooms and let saute until they cook down a bit.. When mushrooms have cooked down add the rest of the ingredients except for the bread crumbs and cheese sauce. Let everything cook for a few minutes until all the flavors blend together. To assemble: Place seeded bell peppers into a 9 x 13 pan. Fill each pepper generously with the filling. Top each pepper with bread crumbs. bake in a 350 degree oven or 35 to 40 minutes. If you would like your pepper softer and a bit blistered then bake longer. link to the no oil plant based cheese sauce: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=y5Bx5z3m6Yg&t=120s Connect with me: * INSTAGRAM: http://instagram.com/chewonvegan * TWITTER: https://twitter.com/chewonvegan * FACEBOOK: https://www.facebook.com/chewonvegan CHECK OUT MY WEBSITE: http://chewonvegan.com/ Try watching the following documentaries: -Game Changers: https://gamechangersmovie.com/ - What the Health (2017)​ https://www.netflix.com/watch/8017417... - Forks over knives. https://www.forksoverknives.com/ - Cowspiracy http://www.cowspiracy.com. title: Uncut and in Conversation with -Vegetarianism with Pietro Leemann, Gabriele Eschenazi & John Creaton
description: Just as sustainability has grown to become a central strand in many companies strategies, so the issue of both vegetarianism and veganism is mainstream. In 2018, Just Eat noted that there had been a 987% increase in demand for vegetarian dishes and it classified veganism as a top consumer trend in 2018/19. Many operators have tripled the number of vegan meals that it does offer in response to this move. This rise in attitudes is reflected in developments in the food market, as consumers’ dietary trends catch the attention of the supply side. The current rise in veganism brings with itself a general interest to eat less meat. For many people, this will be one of the major decisions that they will make during their lives. It is a very conscious move from eating traditional diets to becoming a vegan or even just a vegetarian. We were therefore delighted to talk to 3 leading players to learn their perspectives: Pietro Leeman – Italy’s only Michelin Starred Vegetarian Chef. In 1989 he opened his own restaurant “Joia” in Milan–the first vegetarian restaurant in Europe to receive a Michelin star in 1996–thereby creating a new style of “natural cuisine. Gabriele Eschenazi – Journalist and co-founder of the event “The Vegetarian Chance” John Creaton – Co-founder of The Vegan Arborist. Vegan Arborist is a New Investment Portal Aiming to Unite Plant-Based Business and Individuals

title: Vegan Pasta Salad with Homemade Italian Dressing
description: Vegan Pasta Salad is the perfect potluck side dish, filled with olives, peppers, red onion, roasted red peppers, grape tomatoes, and homemade Italian dressing. REFRIGERATE any leftovers for 2-3 days. We recommend using all of it sooner than later if you're using raw garlic. You could also use a 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder. Both kids and adults will love it! It's colorful and flavorful. Kids tend to pick out meat and cheese. This recipe is naturally vegan and can also be made gluten-free by using gluten-free pasta. You could serve your favorite plant-based meat and cheese on the side for guests to add in if they'd like or even add in if you prefer. INGREDIENTS FOR VEGAN PASTA SALAD Rotini Pasta - To get a healthy mix of whole grains in we use whole wheat pasta in addition to the traditional tri-color pasta. Roasted Red Peppers - We make our homemade roasted red peppers, they're fresh and flavorful! Green Bell Pepper - Peppers add a bit of crunch and color. You could also use other bell pepper colors. Red Onions - These onions not only add a pop of purple, but they also give a strong sharp flavor that pairs well with salads. Tomatoes - Grape tomatoes or Sprinkles Tomatoes are best. We halve grape tomatoes, sprinkles are smaller, but if choking is a risk for you we recommend slicing them as well. Black Olives - These add a salty flavor and can be a bit strong in flavor from the brine. We always drain them and give them a rinse as well. Italian Dressing - You could use your favorite store-bought dressing, but we highly recommend trying our homemade Italian dressing recipe if you have time! title: Recipe - Vegan - Plant-Based Chocolate Cake - Organic Food - Italian Recipe - Delicious - From Italy
description: This cake cost me about 1 euro and 80 cents. It rughly weighs 330g and has 1,212 kcal. It means that if you eat 100g of it you'll be taking in about 367 kcal. So, it's a pretty nutritious cake. But let's talk about the ingredients. 130g - organic flour (1.80 euros/kilo = 0.25 euros) - 468 kcal 60g - organic sugar (2 euros/kilo = 0.12 euros) - 222 kcal 2 spoons or 20g - organic sunflower oil (about 0.12 euros) - 180 kcal 60g - organic dark chocolate (1.80 euros/100g = 1.10 euros) - 342 kcal 9g - organic natural instantaneous yeast (Potassium bitartrate + Sodium bicarbonate) 2 euros/100g = 0.18 euros Now that you have all the ingredients you can put them into a bowl. I first put the flour (130g), then the sugar (60g), then I put the yeast (9g), and 2 spoons of oil. As you can see, adding together the weight of every single ingredient does 279g and not 330g which is the actual weight of the cake. That's because we need the magic ingredient: water. Add water very slowly while mixing until it becomes a thick cream. Then put it into a baking pan, add the chocolate you previously cut into little cubes on top, and put it inside a preheated oven until it solidifies. 180 degees Celsious will be fine. My cake is tiny, and took 18 minutes to cook, but it also depends on how big the cake is and the type of oven you are using.

title: Let's Go to Italy! Top 9 Free Eggplant Parmigiana | Around the World in 80 Plates with Leslie Durso
description: Whether you're allergic to wheat, dairy, soy, egg, fish, shellfish, sesame, peanut, or tree nut, this week, Leslie Durso is taking us to Italy to teach us how to make her Top 9 Free Eggplant Parmigiana! Where do you want Leslie to take us next? Let us know below in the comments. What is Parmigiana? Parmigiana, Italian, also called parmigiana di melanzane or melanzane alla parmigiana, is an Italian dish made with a shallow or deep-fried sliced eggplant filling, layered with cheese and tomato sauce, then baked. The origin of the dish is claimed by both the Southern regions of Campania and Sicily RECIPE IN THE COMMENTS BELOW **Meet Leslie Durso ** A vegan chef and wellness expert, Leslie is the glowing face of a fresher plant-based movement that refuses to compromise style for sustainability. Once simply known as Leslie the lab girl with Bill Nye the science guy, Leslie took her love of education and shifted from science to plant based food. Determined to inspire beneficial life-long eating habits for everyone, her show Accidentally Vegan can be seen on FABFITFUNTV and she regularly appears on The Doctors, Food Network, Discovery Channel, Hallmark Channel, and written for VegNews, CNN.com, Redbook, Glamour, and more, evangelizing the benefits of a mindful lifestyle, delivered with her trademark blend of sunshiney sophistication and girl-next-door relatability.

title: Vegan Salad Dressing recipe | EASY ITALIAN SALAD DRESSING
description: STOP buying salad dressing that is packed with preservatives. You can make your own healthy salad dressing with just a few simple ingredients you may already have on hand. Try this tonight, and I bet you will NEVER go back! This particular dressing is similar to ITALIAN DRESSING. Salad Dressing Ingredients: - salt and pepper - 2-3 tbsp lemon juice - 2 tbsp dijon mustard - 1/4 cup olive oil Garden Salad Ingredients: - lettuce (romaine or iceberg) - red onion - cucumber - tomato - carrot - olives Directions: 1. to make your salad dressing, combine all ingredients in a jar and shake to emulsify 2. to make your salad, chop and prep all ingredients and place in a bowl 3. pour over your salad dressing and serve! title: HOW TO COOK OIL-FREE VEGAN » Water Saute, Broth Saute, Oven Roast - ALL OIL FREE!
channel: The Whole Food Plant Based Cooking Show
description: ★★★ BECOME A SUPPORTING MEMBER! ★★★ Help us keep this show free for everyone by becoming a supporting member! » JOIN MEMBERSHIP: https://community.plantbasedcookingshow.com/membership Cooking without oil strikes many people as either a crazy notion or one that would be terribly difficult. "How do you caramelize onions?" they ask or, "What about roasting vegetables?". In this show we walk through an in depth demonstration of how to water sauté: caramelizing onions without oil, veggie broth sauté: using homemade vegetable broth instead of water to sauté, and oven roasting vegetables with veggie broth instead of oil. Oil Free Vegan Cooking is a rapidly growing trend as more people realize there is no need to pour the empty calories of refined oil onto your tasty plant based food! title: Olive Oil and Wine from Terre Di San Vito, Polignano mare, Puglia, Italy
description: Love and passion are what drive us here at Terre Di San Vito. We started with a small plot of land and just a few vines, olive trees and a plot for vegetables. We soon realised this rust-coloured soil was the perfect environment for growing exceptionally flavoursome grapes and produce. Over the past 15 years, we have increased the size of our farm to 45 hectares and this has enabled us to extend the vineyards, build a cellar and increase the number of olive trees whilst still maintaining traditional values and sustainable methods for working the land. With respect for the past and a love of food and wine, our family have lovingly restored and developed this land. We have built a new summer house where you can experience our exceptional wine-tasting sessions. A shop with all our traditional produce and where we add the finishing touches to our wine bottles. A chicken coop, a drove of donkeys, a flock of goats and a muster of beautiful peacocks are a new and welcomed addition to the land. You must come and visit us – it is indeed a place where work and nature live in perfect harmony. Our produce Our signature products are: wine, olive oil and marinated vegetables. We use traditional, Southern Italian methods for collecting our olives and grapes as well as in the making of our sundried tomatoes and vegetables marinated in olive oil. These Southern Italian methods really mean getting the staff and the whole family together: sisters, brothers, aunts, uncles and cousins – including the family from England (timing holidays to coincide with harvest time). With the Mediterranean sun shining over the region, our vineyards grow an incredible range of grapes: Malbec, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Primitivo for our full-bodied flavoursome red wines. Sauvignon Blanc and Bombino Nero for our mild white and delicately sweet Rosé. We begin the harvest at sunrise (after a full English breakfast) while the temperature is still cool. Zio Franco (owner) and Salac (head groundsman) give strict instructions about remembering to put the grapes ‘carefully’ in to the crates – no throwing or they will blister and begin to ferment! They give the same instructions every year. Then the picking and cutting begins. We pick until the afternoon and it is back breaking work but we love it! Our produce give you a real authentic taste of Puglia. At Terre Di San Vito, we grow and use Coratina, Ogliarola and Favolosa olives and meticulous attention is given to the production process, in particular, harvesting by hand and then cold-pressing for optimal flavour. This Olive Oil can be found in Waitrose stores across the UK. It is this rich olive oil which we also use to marinate our delicious sundried tomatoes. The cultivation of the grapes and olives is carried out near Polignano a Mare, mostly in the Hamlet of San Vito. The farm has received recognition from slowfood.com as an exemplary model of quality-based farming, knowledge recovery and traditional production techniques. The team We pour the very essence of Italian craftsmanship, traditional Apulian methods and love into our authentic. None of this would be possible without the Terre Di San Vito team and family. We strongly believe that you cannot make great produce without a great team and great passion. Even though we are a small family farm, we are fortunate to have a wonderfully hard-working, loyal and experienced team and willing family members! Over the years, we have learnt together and discovered our own way of doing things. Sharing common values and having the same goals is our recipe for success. Then we have us, the UK family! We are based in the UK but we spent our childhood in Italy. Every school holiday – off we went to see the family and enjoy the company of our 35 cousins. We loved every minute! A small farm we may be – a small family – no! Now as adults, our love for our family and traditions has remained. Johnny De Napoli is Franco’s nephew – our dad was Franco’s big brother and to say our dad loved his family, wine and food is not an understatement and we share that same love and passion. Johnny is a media executive who celebrates his Italian roots at every opportunity. He relishes drinking wine and eating the mouth-watering, traditional foods of his Italian heritage by opening the distribution of Terre Di San Vito’s product range to the UK and beyond. Sharing with you, the delights of Terre Di San Vito is his pleasure. Whenever you are in Puglia – come and see us – you can meet the team, share our food and explore our wines! From Terre Di San Vito, with love ❤️

title: SPESA ESSELUNGA : *DELIRIO TOTALE per CORONAVIRUS* - Video di Giorgio Immesi
description: SEGUIMI SU INSTAGRAM: https://www.instagram.com/giorgioimmesi/ ISCRIVITI AL CANALE YOUTUBE: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC5neCcSxlXkQFQz7yS9Jz6w -------------- ⬇️⬇️⬇️ 📍 PRODOTTI CHE CONSIGLIO: ✅ Vitamina B12 : https://amzn.to/3c8XBNB ✅ Multivitaminico: https://amzn.to/2RPsJd3 ✅ Proteine Vegane: https://amzn.to/38JDu6o ✅ Spirulina Bio: https://amzn.to/2OgHaoe ✅ Probiotici Vegan: https://amzn.to/2S51074 STRUMENTI CHE UTILIZZO: ✅ Videocamera che utilizzo per fare video: https://amzn.to/37Aceac ✅ Microfono per videocamera: https://amzn.to/312c9d4 ✅ Treppiede che utilizzo per fare video: https://amzn.to/3aF63DE ✅ Zaino tecnico per viaggi/video: https://amzn.to/3aR8zHh ————— Questo nuovo format è dedicato a tutte quei vegani che, ogni giorno, sono alla ricerca di nuovi prodotti cruelty free, Nella terza puntata ho fatto la spesa all' ESSELUNGA. Esselunga è distribuita in gran parte nel nord Italia, ma quanto sarà vegan? Offre dei prodotti per chi ha deciso di non mangiare più animali e derivati? Sono rimasto completamente scioccato dalla quantità di prodotti vegan. Ho assaggiato una marea di nuovi prodotti, sono veramente felice che ci siano realtà come queste, ogni tanto per i vegani arrivano delle GIOIE. Il coronavirus ha svuotato tutti i supermercati, il panico si è letteralmente sprigionato in Italia. Ovviamente io la spesa l'ho fatta qualche giorno prima, ma solo se guarderai il video fino alla fine o se leggerai questa descrizione lo capirai... Questo video non è una sponsorizzazione a ESSELUNGA e a nessun altro prodotto. Tutto ciò che vedete è puro desiderio di scoprire nuovi sapori vegetali e farli conoscere a tutti coloro che hanno scelto questa filosofia di vita. ——————— Il veganismo non è una scelta basata sull'alimentazione vegana e basta, anzi, il veganismo è una scelta etica intelligente in grado di fornire interessanti risvolti positivi, sia in termini di salute e sia in termini di conoscenza. La scelta vegana è in grado di garantire tutti quei nutrienti che permettono ad ogni essere umano di vivere in forma e in salute. Il vegano imbruttito è uno stile di vita.

title: Is this a CHEAP SUPERMARKET?
description: With the question in mind, where is the cheapest supermarket in England?! We have decided to check out Lidl and see if it is any cheaper than our usual supermarket. We will visit many more supermarkets but this is our experience with Lidl in the UK but we also compared it with the Italian Lidl. title: SPESA CARREFOUR : TANTISSIMI NUOVI PRODOTTI - Vlog di Giorgio Immesi
description: 💁🏻‍♂️ In questo video ho fatto la spesa vegan da Carrefour, ho scoperto tantissimi prodotti, mi saranno piaciuti?.. —————— 💁🏻‍♂️ Se vuoi sostenere questo canale lascia un LIKE, un COMMENTO e ISCRIVITI al CANALE attivando la CAMPANELLA. ⬇️⬇️⬇️ 📍 PRODOTTI CHE CONSIGLIO: ✅ Vitamina B12 : https://amzn.to/3c8XBNB ✅ Multivitaminico: https://amzn.to/2RPsJd3 ✅ Proteine Vegane: https://amzn.to/38JDu6o ✅ Spirulina Bio: https://amzn.to/2OgHaoe ✅ Probiotici Vegan: https://amzn.to/2S51074 STRUMENTI CHE UTILIZZO: ✅ Videocamera che utilizzo per fare video: https://amzn.to/37Aceac ✅ Microfono per videocamera: https://amzn.to/312c9d4 ✅ Treppiede che utilizzo per fare video: https://amzn.to/3aF63DE ✅ Zaino tecnico per viaggi/video: https://amzn.to/3aR8zHh ————— spesa Naturasì: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mYeF1dWH3pw ————— Questo nuovo format è dedicato a tutte quei vegani che, ogni giorno, sono alla ricerca di nuovi prodotti cruelty free e fanno la spesa vegan. In questa puntata ho visitato il Carrefour vicino casa mia. Carrefour è distribuito in gran parte dell' Italia, ma quanto sarà vegan? Offre dei prodotti per chi ha deciso di non mangiare più animali e derivati? Sono rimasto completamente scioccato dalla quantità di prodotti vegan. Ho assaggiato una marea di nuovi prodotti, sono veramente felice che ci siano realtà come queste, per alcuni prodotti invece sono rimasto parecchio deluso. ogni tanto per i vegani arrivano delle GIOIE. Ho trovato finalmente i tanto chiacchierati bastoncini vegan al sapore di mare, ho trovato il cioccolato al latte di cocco, cioccolato vegano, ho trovato la bresaola vegana e lo speck vegano e moltissimi altri prodotti di vari marchi. Questo video non è una sponsorizzazione a Carrefour e a nessun altro prodotto. Tutto ciò che vedete è puro desiderio di scoprire nuovi sapori vegetali e farli conoscere a tutti coloro che hanno scelto questa filosofia di vita. ——————— Il veganismo non è una scelta basata sull'alimentazione vegana e basta, anzi, il veganismo è una scelta etica intelligente in grado di fornire interessanti risvolti positivi, sia in termini di salute e sia in termini di conoscenza. La scelta vegana è in grado di garantire tutti quei nutrienti che permettono ad ogni essere umano di vivere in forma e in salute. Il vegano imbruttito è uno stile di vita.

title: The Vegan Roadie: Ciao Italia! (S03E01) SICILY
description: In this first episode of The Vegan Roadie Season 3: Ciao Italia! Dustin explores all of the plant based eats that Sicily has to offer. He starts things off with a visit from his friend Miyoko Schinner as she helps him get the lay of the land in Ortigia. From there Dustin finds more eats in Catania and Modica. Pasta (all the pasta!), the best cannoli on the planet, famous chocolate, fried dough balls and more! Wrapped up with Dustin's signature 5 Ingredient Challenge - click play and get ready for a feast! The Vegan Roadie - S3: Ciao Italia! title: VEGAN FOOD TO EAT IN MILAN ITALY | TRAVEL GUIDE
description: Join me as I show you what vegan food to eat in Milan, Italy! Want to know what to see, eat and do in Milan? In this video, I will show you Milan top attractions, such as Duomo Cathedral, what to do in Milan on a weekend, and best of all, where to eat vegan food in Milan. One of Italy's most vegan friendly cities. As a vegan and plant based foodie, here's the food reviews I do in Milan: one of the best vegan tiramisus ever, vegan Naples style pizza, a vegan four-course meal at a vegan Michelin star restaurant, Joia, raw vegan chocolate desserts and how to get to Lake Como from the Milan City Center. Let's enjoy Milan as it was before the covid-19 pandemic!

title: Factory boosts pasta production as demand soars
description: (29 Mar 2020) LEAD IN : As the new coronavirus continues to spread across much of the world, in many places people are panic buying store cupboard essentials to get them through local lockdowns. Pasta is one popular commodity that is high demand and factories in Italy are increasing production in an effort to keep up. STORY-LINE: Pasta is an essential staple in any Italian kitchen. Boil in hot water, then drain and add a delicious sauce - what could be simpler? But demand has soared in the wake of the coronavirus pandemic. Italy has been on nationwide lockdown since 9 March. And at supermarkets, shoppers fill their carts high with packets of pasta. For Italians, pasta isn't simply crisis food, but rather an essential in their way of life, explains one shopper. "I bought pasta to make with beans, large and small, so I bought both small and large pasta depending on the beans, then I bought short pasta and long pasta, so spaghetti, but also rigatoni, penne, that is, the pasta that we need in Italy." This supermarket in Campobasso, in the southern region of Molise, saw its pasta shelves completely empty in the first week of March. "We saw an increase in pasta sales, in particular, semolina pasta, of about 11 points," explains Elizabeth Ciricillo, consumables manager at the Conad Supermarket of Campobasso. Since then, shelves have been restocked regularly and consumers haven't had to deal with a pasta shortage. But at La Molisana, the fourth largest pasta producer in Italy, the increase in demand has drained their stock. "Our dry pasta production capacity on average reaches 410 tonnes of pasta in 24 hours," says explains Flavio Ferro, Chief Operating Officer and Partner at La Molisana. "Our dry pasta production capacity in the last month and a half was particularly impacted by this world epidemiological crisis – as a result 70 percent of our stock was used up. In a normal situation we would send out 500 tonnes of pasta every 24 hours, and now we are running at 800 tonnes per day." And pasta hoarding isn't just an Italian behaviour: in fact, according to La Molisana, the phenomenon repeated itself elsewhere as the virus began to spread: "We first saw a hoarding run which brought us to a 20 percent increase of our daily shipment, when the country was put in lockdown we reached up to 60 percent increase in our daily shipment compared to last year at the same time," says Ferro. "As the virus made its way to all the reception points of our pasta we saw exactly the same situation repeat, from the United States, to Japan and South America where we usually ship a part of our pasta goods." With demand increasing across the world, this company is now forced to make some choices. "In some situations we are forced to think in terms of a rationing system. In fact we have decided to decrease the number of types of pasta from 60 to a little over 10," says Ferro While a shortage in pasta is not to be expected anywhere near Campobasso, where the company is based, more medical equipment is needed. So the Ferro family has donated three lung ventilators to the Operating Block of the Intensive Care Unit at Cardarelli Hospital in Campobasso, as a way to give back to their community. Meanwhile supermarkets in countries like France and the United Kingdom have also started to ration pasta. But Italian families still have plenty in enjoy - lockdown or not, they continue to share moments together over their shared love, simple pasta dishes: "Are you having it with cheese? How are you guys having it?," asks the Provera family in an online video call with other friends and relatives. title: Posta Italbar in Mississauga makes clam-shaped pizza
description: Posta Italbar is an Italian restaurant in Port Credit that makes lots of fresh pizza and pasta, but this one menu item is completely next-level. This pizza sandwiched is shaped like a clam, stuffed with burrata and vegetables and baked in the oven. Find it at 31 Lakeshore Rd E. Read more: https://www.blogto.com/restaurants/posta-italbar-mississauga/
